svg.title: MN 111 — Anupada Sutta — Sequentially
svg.desc: A diagram of MN 111. Sāriputta enters each of nine successive attainments, and within each one sequentially discerns the distinctive factors of that attainment together with eleven universal mind-states (contact, feeling, perception, intention, mind, desire, resolution, energy, mindfulness, equanimity, attention). Each state is observed arising, persisting, and passing away. The visual repetition of the same eleven-state grid in every slice is the central argument of the diagram. Cessation of perception and what is felt is reached, the taints are exhausted, and the Buddha praises Sāriputta as having attained mastery in noble virtue, collectedness, wisdom, and liberation, and as the one who keeps the unsurpassed wheel of the Dhamma rolling.

g.text: arose known · remained known · passed away known
“For half a month, Sāriputta cultivated insight through sequential discernment of mental states.”
THE ANUPADA TEMPLATE — WHAT SĀRIPUTTA DISCERNED IN EACH ATTAINMENT
In every attainment, the same template repeats:
distinctive factors + the eleven universal mind-states : seeing how they arise → persist → pass
what makes this attainment what it is
e.g. vitakka · vicāra · pīti · sukha · cittekaggatā
present in every attainment from the first jhāna through nothingness
these eleven appear in every slice — and each one is discerned
“not having been, they come to be; having been, they vanish”
each chip in B is watched
secluded from sensual pleasures and unwholesome states · vivicceva kāmehi · vivicca akusalehi dhammehi
each one sequentially discerned · arose, remained, passed
with the settling of reflection and examination · vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā
arose known · remained known · passed away known
with the fading of desire for uplifting joy · pītiyā ca virāgā
arose known · remained known · passed away known
with abandoning pleasure and pain; with the settling of mental pleasure and displeasure
sukhassa ca pahānā · dukkhassa ca pahānā · somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā
arose known · remained known · passed away known
surpassing perceptions of form, aware that “space is boundless · ananto ākāso”
+ THE UNIVERSAL ELEVEN — still all present, still all discerned
arose known · remained known · passed away known
surpassing the base of boundless space, aware that “consciousness is boundless · anantaṁ viññāṇaṁ”
+ THE UNIVERSAL ELEVEN — still all present, still all discerned
arose known · remained known · passed away known
surpassing the base of boundless consciousness, aware that “there is nothing · natthi kiñcī”
+ THE UNIVERSAL ELEVEN — still all present, still all discerned
arose known · remained known · passed away known
Base of Neither Perception nor Non-Perception
Having emerged mindful, he contemplated the states that had passed, ceased, and changed.
Cessation of Perception and What Is Felt
saññāvedayitanirodha · with the complete surpassing of the base of neither perception nor non-perception
Having seen with wisdom, the taints get completely exhausted
paññāya cassa disvā āsavā parikkhīṇā honti
Emerged mindful · contemplated states that had passed, ceased, and changed
— of Sāriputta indeed it is rightly said
not an inheritor of material things
The unsurpassed wheel of the Dhamma
is kept rolling rightly by Sāriputta
